about.title: From idea to working product
about.description: I focus on building modern web applications using React, TypeScript, and Next.js, with strong attention to code quality, performance, and usability. I specialize in simplifying complex problems and turning them into intuitive user interfaces. I work closely with designers and stakeholders to ensure that delivered features are consistent, thoughtful, and truly useful.
highlights: In team environments, I focus on clear communication, solid organization, and continuous improvements that make collaboration smoother and more predictable.

projects.easterEggModalBody: It is, of course, a UFO abducting a cow. Not just any UFO either, but one that clearly decided this portfolio needed one more dramatic subplot. The cow did not approve this roadmap item, but the cosmos rarely waits for stakeholder sign-off.

items.title: FUNKOLOGI
items.description: A production-oriented website and resource library for a Swedish psychology practice, combining a polished public experience with a protected Firebase-backed admin workflow for publishing downloadable PDF materials.
paragraphs: FUNKOLOGI is a Swedish psychology practice focused on neuropsychology, autistic children, and family support. The starting point was straightforward: the client had previously built her own WordPress site, but the result did not communicate the level of professionalism, calm, and trust needed for sensitive specialist work.

I treated the project as both a redesign and a small content platform. The public site was rebuilt around clearer positioning, SEO, accessibility, contact paths, practitioner credibility, and a searchable resource library organized into practical material categories such as energy and stress, structure and routines, social skills, and feelings and demand sensitivity.

The most important operational piece was the protected admin workflow. I built an allowlisted admin area with Auth.js Credentials authentication, Firebase Authentication, short-lived sessions, idle logout handling, and server-side validation so the client can upload PDFs, edit metadata, manage categories and keywords, and delete resources without touching code.

Behind the scenes, Firebase Storage holds the PDF files and generated preview images, while Firestore is the source of truth for titles, descriptions, keywords, file metadata, storage paths, download tokens, page counts, and preview URLs. The upload flow also processes PDFs, extracts page counts, generates first-page PNG previews, and revalidates the public category pages after content changes.

The final result is a maintainable, mobile-first Next.js application that pairs a polished marketing experience with a production-oriented publishing workflow. The business outcome is simple: the client now has a credible website and a practical self-managed resource library for downloadable materials.
bulletPoints: Rebuilt a self-made WordPress presence into a custom Next.js App Router experience with stronger trust, clearer positioning, SEO metadata, and structured content delivery.
Designed a calm, accessible, mobile-first interface for families seeking specialist psychological support around neuropsychology, autism, and everyday family life.
Created a searchable public resource library with generated category routes, keyword suggestions, PDF cards, preview images, file metadata, open links, and direct download actions.
Built a protected admin area for allowlisted users with Auth.js Credentials authentication, Firebase Authentication, short-lived JWT sessions, and idle logout handling.
Implemented server actions for material upload, metadata updates, deletion, validation, and cache revalidation after content changes.
Added a PDF processing pipeline that extracts page counts and generates first-page PNG previews during upload.
Connected Firestore, Firebase Storage, Firebase Admin SDK, and download-token metadata so materials can be managed outside the repository.
Kept static category definitions versioned in TypeScript while merging them with dynamic Firestore documents at request time.

meta.label: Stack
meta.value: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, MUI 7, Firebase, Auth.js

meta.label: Primary goal
meta.value: Trust + searchable PDF library + self-managed publishing

items.title: Northstar Commerce
items.description: A portfolio case study for an e-commerce operations dashboard that brings revenue, conversion, order flow, acquisition performance, customer insights, and operational risk into one workspace.
paragraphs: Northstar Commerce is a portfolio project for an e-commerce operations dashboard. It was shaped as a product-style workspace rather than a generic analytics demo, with a clear primary user: an e-commerce operations manager who needs to monitor storefront performance and act on operational issues quickly.

The product story centers on a practical daily workflow: review top-line storefront health, spot movement in acquisition channels, retention, conversion quality, refunds, fulfillment delays, and order risk, then drill into the records that need follow-up. The main promise is one workspace for moving from signal to action without losing context.

The Overview Dashboard works as an executive and operational summary. It covers KPI cards for net revenue, orders, average order value, and conversion rate, then adds revenue trends, retention and refund signals, customer mix, acquisition channels, category demand, loyalty tiers, channel efficiency, and risk-oriented insight widgets.

The Order Intelligence view extends the dashboard into a record-level operations workspace. It includes saved views, explicit filters, quick presets, summary cards for tracked orders and risk exposure, a searchable data table, and a watchlist panel for current issues and follow-up notes.

Account and Preferences were reframed as real product views rather than placeholder pages. The final experience includes workspace identity, reporting preferences, visible metrics, alerts, digest emails, and shared dashboard settings, while keeping the implementation portfolio-scoped with demo data and preview-only feedback.
bulletPoints: Framed the product around a specific primary user: an e-commerce operations manager responsible for revenue, conversion health, order flow, channel performance, and customer follow-up.
Designed the workflow to move from high-level storefront signals into operational detail: overview metrics, trend interpretation, record-level investigation, and workspace settings.

Built an Overview Dashboard with net revenue, orders, average order value, conversion rate, revenue trends, retention, refunds, acquisition channels, customer mix, category demand, and loyalty indicators.
Created an Order Intelligence workspace with saved views, explicit filter controls, quick presets, risk summaries, searchable operational records, and a watchlist for active follow-up.
Reframed Account and Preferences as supporting product screens for workspace identity, reporting preferences, visible metrics, alerts, digest emails, and shared dashboards.
Refined the visual direction with a restrained data palette, stronger dashboard hierarchy, contextual headers, subtle brand watermark treatment, and product-oriented e-commerce copy.
Improved readability across light and dark themes, with responsive summary views for mobile and denser charts and full data grids reserved for tablet and desktop screens.
Kept the scope portfolio-appropriate with demo data and preview-only actions while delivering a clean production build and lint pass.

meta.label: Stack
meta.value: Next.js, React, TypeScript, MUI, Chart.js, Sass modules, NextAuth

slides.title: Storefront overview
slides.caption: The opening dashboard frames Northstar as a daily operations workspace, pairing a product hero and today's signal panel with prioritized KPI cards for revenue, orders, average order value, and conversion.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce overview dashboard with hero panel, signal card, and storefront KPI metrics

slides.title: Revenue and retention trend
slides.caption: This trend section compares revenue momentum across Paid Search, Email, and Organic channels, while side cards keep returning customer share, checkout completion, and refund rate visible for fast interpretation.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce revenue trend chart with retention, checkout, and refund summary cards

slides.title: Customer and risk insights
slides.caption: The insight widgets use quick-read donut charts to summarize lifecycle mix, acquisition channel share, order risk breakdown, and loyalty tier distribution for an operations manager scanning the storefront.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce insight widgets for customer mix, acquisition channels, order risk, and loyalty tiers

slides.title: Action signals and category mix
slides.caption: This section turns performance monitoring into next-step prioritization with short operational signals, a channel efficiency radar chart, and a category revenue view for merchandising follow-up.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce action signals with channel efficiency radar chart and category revenue chart

slides.title: Weekly demand signals
slides.caption: The demand chart balances gross orders, recovered carts, and churn risk over time, helping the dashboard show whether weekly demand is strengthening or creating follow-up risk.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce weekly demand signals bar chart with gross orders, recovered carts, and churn risk

slides.title: Order Intelligence workspace
slides.caption: The operational workspace brings saved views, active filters, quick presets, search, a scrollable order table, and watchlist notes together for record-level investigation and follow-up.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce Order Intelligence workspace with filters, searchable order table, and operational notes

slides.title: Workspace preferences
slides.caption: The Preferences screen turns settings into a product surface, covering visible metrics, alert channels, digest cadence, shared dashboards, and the workspace status summary.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce preferences screen with visible metrics, notifications, and workspace status

slides.title: Account and reporting setup
slides.caption: The Account screen completes the product story with workspace identity, profile details, saved views, alert coverage, digest preferences, and preview-only account update feedback.
slides.imageAlt: Northstar Commerce account screen with workspace identity, profile settings, and reporting notes
